papers.name: Paper 1 Geographical Themes
papers.coverage_notes: Three geographical themes (one from each theme group).

papers.name: Paper 3 School-based Assessment (Coursework)
papers.coverage_notes: One coursework assignment (max 2000 words) based on fieldwork.

papers.name: Paper 4 Alternative to Coursework
papers.coverage_notes: Written alternative for centres unable to run Paper 3.

assessment_objectives.name: Demonstrate geographical knowledge
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge of locations, places, processes and environments at a variety of scales.
classification_hints: Items asking for place knowledge, terminology, recall of processes.

assessment_objectives.name: Demonstrate understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate understanding of the relationships between people and their environments.
classification_hints: Items requiring explanation, linking cause and effect, geographical reasoning.

assessment_objectives.name: Apply knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Apply knowledge and understanding to interpret, analyse and evaluate geographical information and issues.
classification_hints: Items requiring interpretation of maps, data, photographs; application of concepts.

assessment_objectives.name: Select, use and evaluate skills
assessment_objectives.description: Select, use and evaluate a range of geographical skills and techniques.
classification_hints: Items requiring map skills, graph/chart interpretation, statistical analysis, fieldwork.

question_styles.name: Data response
question_styles.description: Interpretation of geographical data, maps, photographs.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or single-step application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when a teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: CAIE IGCSE Geography command words (state, describe, explain, suggest, analyse, evaluate) are defined in Section 4 of the syllabus.
marking_guidance.notes: Single-tier qualification (grades A*-G). Candidates take Paper 3 (Coursework) or Paper 4 (Alternative to Coursework), not both.
